title: The question your patent cannot answer: Freedom to operate in India
description: For many businesses, the most dangerous intellectual property assumption is also the most common: “We have a patent, therefore we are free to sell our product.”Indian patent law does not work that way. A patent principally confers an exclusionary right against third parties, namely the right to prevent others from exploiting the patented invention. It does not guarantee that the patentee may itself commercially practice the invention without infringing earlier or broader third-party rights.That
